creator: web@umich.edu
text:
It is right there in the Subject: line -- "not Xorg/x11 related".
  
I think it is a virtual terminal thing, but I'm likely wrong.

It is at console (vt0), and I moved to vt1 to verify without having
any console related confusion.
  
I'd just recently loaded (memstick) FreeBSD-15.0-CURRENT-amd64
via June 20th snapshot (I needed the patch from Bugzilla 270707)
and I didn't want to bother recompiling the kernel.  I figured
that 15.0-CURRENT would have the patch needed to get past the
initial hang during installation.

The native T16 keyboard responds within three or five seconds.
This makes it frustrating to type.
  
Logged into root and that worked, but it took forever.
  
Then I plugged in an external USB keyboard, and that worked
just great like the native keyboard should have worked.
  
BTW, Bugzilla 270707 also required me to "unset" hint.uart.1.at
at boot time, then boot.  Later I commented out that line in
the /boot/device.hints file.
